macro_segments.title: Chametz Buried Under Rubble and the Requirement of Bitul

micro_segments.title: Chametz buried by rubble is considered burned; Rabban Gamliel's stricter view based on dog-digging depth
Rav Chisda's ruling that bitul must still be performed despite burial, with Rashi's explanation about potential excavation during Chag
Three-tefach standard for dog-digging depth and calculation of measurement
Rav Acha's question comparing chametz burial to money storage using Shmuel's principle
Rav Ashi's distinction: three tefachim required for chametz due to smell, but only one tefach for money (hiddenness from view)

macro_segments.title: One Who Eats Terumah Chametz on Pesach: Payment Obligations and Hana'ah

micro_segments.title: Introduction to terumah status: sanctified nature and prohibition on Pesach; comparison to hekdesh
Mishnah ruling: one who eats terumah chametz on Pesach must pay principal and fifth if unintentional; exempt from payment if intentional
Rashi's explanation: terumah chametz is prohibited (assur b'hana'ah) but still subject to terumah restitution laws
Spiritual vs. monetary perspective: atonement through restitution for intentional violators; קים ליה בדרבה מיניה principle

macro_segments.title: Payment According to Measure or According to Value in Terumah Cases

micro_segments.title: Opening question: whether restitution is לפי מידה (by quantity) or לפי דמים (by monetary value)
Principle that hekdesh always favors the injured party: if value decreased, pay original value; if value increased, pay according to type of liability
Case of dried figs and fresh dates: Rav Yosef's example showing blessing for better substitution
Abaye's resolution: payment is לפי דמים (by value) but blessing comes from ease of resale, not absolute value increase

macro_segments.title: Terumah Chametz: Whether It Has Value and Payment Obligations

micro_segments.title: Question: if terumah chametz on Pesach is worthless (לפי דמים), why pay קרן וחומש at all?
Distinction: Rabi Yosi HaGalili holds chametz has value even on Pesach; mainstream view holds it has no hana'ah
Rabbi Akiva vs. Rabbi Yochanan ben Nuri dispute: one exempts from payment (קים ליה בדרבה מיניה), other obligates restitution
Harkonus ben Hakanas equates Yom Kippur to Shabbat for restitution purposes, establishing קים ליה בדרבה מיניה in all cases of death penalty
Debate continued: whether terumah chametz can become holy; if given from chametz itself at outset, it never attains kedushah status
Rabbi Akiva and Rabbi Yochanan ben Nuri both hold chametz assur b'hana'ah; they dispute whether payment is לפי דמים or לפי מידה
Abaye's clarification: all opinions hold chametz assur b'hana'ah; dispute is only on measure vs. value method of payment

macro_segments.title: General Terumah Restitution: Minimum Quantities and Values Required

micro_segments.title: Tanna Kamma: one who eats kezayit of terumah must pay principal and fifth; Abba Saul requires value of at least perutah
Tanna Kamma focuses on minimum consumption (kezayit); derives from פסוק emphasis on achilah (eating)
Abba Saul focuses on giving (nesinat) requirement; minimum value is perutah based on non-halachic giving of worthless items
Tanna Kamma addresses Abba Saul's perutah with term 'davar hara'uy lihyot kodesh': item must be fit to be sanctified
Abba Saul addresses Tanna Kamma's kezayit with prat l'mazik: achilah excludes destruction; doesn't negate quantity requirement

macro_segments.title: Less Than Kezayit: When Obligation to Pay Principal but Not Fifth Applies

micro_segments.title: Baraita: one eating less than kezayit pays principal but not fifth; clarification of conditions
Case resolution: item must be worth at least perutah; since it lacks kezayit size, fifth is not paid
Question whether Abba Saul requires both value (perutah) and size (kezayit) together
Rav Papa's initial position: even Abba Saul requires both perutah and kezayit; retracted upon challenge
Refutation (tiyuvta) from tannaitic sources: Abba Saul requires perutah alone, Tanna Kamma requires kezayit alone—not both

topical_tags.term: payment of keren v'chomesh (principal and fifth)
misah biydei shamayim (death by heavenly hands)
kim lei be'drabba minei (more severe punishment already incurred)
Rabbi Yosi HaGalili (holds chametz has value on Pesach)
Harkonus ben Hakanas (equates Yom Kippur to Shabbat)
davar hara'uy lihyot kodesh (item fit to be sanctified)
terumah temeiah (impure terumah; allowed for fuel)
mafresh trumah v'himitzah (separated terumah that became chametz)
